name: University of Toronto
summary: The University of Toronto is one of the most important institutions in the history of deep learning. Geoffrey Hinton's research group at U of T produced AlexNet (2012), the convolutional neural network that ignited the deep learning revolution by winning the ImageNet competition by a record margin. Hinton's students — including Ilya Sutskever, Alex Krizhevsky, and Yann LeCun (postdoc) — went on to co-found or lead AI efforts at OpenAI, Google, and Meta. The university's Department of Computer Science and the associated Vector Institute became a global epicenter for neural network research during a period when few other institutions took the approach seriously.
